mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.0 percent indicates severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the high unemployment signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts reflect broad-based consumer demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in the index of industrial production signals improving supply-side capacity utilization that boosts short-term equity momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative 5-day forward returns in >55% of observations during late-cycle expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 225.0 triggers expectations of imminent monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 225.0 are followed by positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms genuine labor market tightening, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity that translates into positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels fail to predict 5-day forward returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the industrial production index during a recessionary phase signals collapsing manufacturing output, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during recessions are followed by positive 20-day returns in >50% of instances.
